A rounded square button with a white/grey body, a thin green inset line outline, and a shadowed bottom edge for depth. Mostly white flat vector style with green accent. Works as a secondary raised button or unselected tab/slot.

A plain solid bright green rounded square with a slightly darker outline and no bevel, gloss, or gradient. Minimal flat vector style. Ideal as the pressed state of the depth_flat button, an active tab, or a colored panel chip.

A bright green rounded square with a lighter glossy top-half highlight and no bottom depth lip. Flat vector cartoon style. Serves as the pressed state of the glossy depth button or a standalone shiny square button.

A rounded square green button with a smooth vertical gradient from light to darker green and a thin outline, flat with no 3D edge. Clean vector UI style. Useful as a pressed gradient button state or simple square UI panel base.

A rounded square made of a white/grey face with a thin green line outline inset near the edge, no depth shading. Minimal flat vector style. Good for outlined secondary buttons, unselected toggles, or empty inventory slots.

A small solid bright green rounded square with a darker outline, the filled but markless state of a green checkbox. Flat vector UI style. Use as a checkbox background awaiting an overlaid checkmark/cross, or a selected color swatch.

A green rounded-square checkbox with a smaller white-outlined green square filling its center, an indeterminate/partially-selected style state. Flat vector UI in greens and white. Good for tri-state checkboxes or selected slot highlights.

A white/grey rounded-square checkbox containing a bold green X/cross mark. Flat vector UI style, white box with green cross. Usable as a declined/off checkbox state or a light-themed close/cancel box.

A white/grey rounded-square checkbox with a smaller solid dark green square in its center, an indeterminate/partial selection state. Flat vector UI style, white with green fill. Good for tri-state checkboxes or partially-complete indicators.

A standalone bold green checkmark/tick glyph with a darker green outline, no background. Small flat vector icon. Use to mark completed tasks, correct answers, confirm buttons, or owned items anywhere in the UI.
A small solid green filled circle glyph with a darker green outline, no background. Tiny flat vector icon. Useful as a bullet point, radio dot, status indicator, map marker, or pagination dot.

A green checkmark/tick glyph with a thick white outline around it, transparent background. Flat vector icon style that stays readable over busy backgrounds. Good for overlay confirmation marks on items, map pins, or HUD notifications.

A solid green circle glyph surrounded by a thick white outline, transparent background. Small flat vector icon designed for legibility over any backdrop. Useful as a status dot, selection marker, or map indicator over game scenes.

A bold green X/cross glyph with a thick white outline, transparent background. Flat vector icon built to read clearly over busy art. Good for close buttons over screenshots, invalid-placement markers, or denial indicators in-world.

A solid green square glyph with a thick white outline, transparent background. Tiny flat vector icon. Usable as a stop icon, selection swatch, legend marker, or shape indicator that stays visible over any background.
A small solid green filled square with a darker green border, no background. Minimal flat vector icon. Works as a stop button glyph, color swatch, bullet, or grid/legend marker in UI.

A slider handle/thumb shaped like a vertical rounded pentagon or house/shield pointing up, bright green with a darker green outline. Flat vector UI style. Grabs onto a horizontal slider track for volume or settings sliders (filename is Kenney's 'hangle' typo for handle).

A long thin horizontal green slider track: a slim green bar with small round caps on each end, drawn in flat vector style. Represents the filled/colored portion of a slider or progress bar. Use as the value-fill track under a slider handle for volume or brightness settings.

A small square section of green slider track with a circular bulge in the middle, designed as a tileable middle segment or notch of a segmented slider. Flat vector style, bright green on darker green bar. Use to build custom-length sliders or stepped/notched tracks.

A wider green slider track segment with a round bulge/node at its center, twice the width of the standard section. Flat vector style in two greens. A tileable piece for assembling longer segmented or notched slider tracks and progress meters.

A long thin horizontal white/grey slider track bar with small rounded end caps, flat vector style. Represents the empty/background portion of a slider or progress bar. Layer the green colored track over this to show the current value.

A small white/grey slider track section with a circular bulge at center, the unfilled counterpart of the green section piece. Flat vector style, soft white/grey. Tileable segment for building empty portions of segmented sliders or step indicators.

A wider white/grey slider track segment with a round node at its center, double-width version of the grey section. Flat vector UI style. Used to tile out longer unfilled slider tracks or notched progress bars in a light theme.

A tall thin vertical green slider track: a slim bright green bar with small rounded caps at top and bottom, flat vector style. The colored/filled track for a vertical slider or gauge. Use for vertical volume sliders, scrollbar fills, or vertical progress meters.

A small square section tile of a green vertical slider track, drawn as a flat vector rounded shape with a darker green outline. It is a middle segment meant to be tiled/stretched to build a vertical slider or scrollbar rail of arbitrary length. Bright green fill, clean flat UI style from the Kenney UI pack.

A taller (16x32) segment of a green vertical slider track with a slight bulge in the middle, flat vector style with dark green border. Used as a tiling middle piece for a colored vertical slider fill or scrollbar rail in game UI, e.g. a volume or progress control.

A full-height thin vertical slider rail in light grey/white, drawn as a slim rounded bar with small circular knobs at both ends, flat vector UI style. Serves as the complete empty track for a vertical slider or scrollbar, over which a colored fill and handle would be placed.

A small 16x16 grey/white section tile of a vertical slider track, flat vector style with a soft outline. It is a repeatable middle segment for building an uncolored (empty) vertical slider or scrollbar rail of any length in game menus.

A 16x32 grey/white vertical slider track segment with a slight central bulge, flat vector UI style. Used as a tiling middle piece for the empty portion of a vertical slider or scrollbar rail, pairing with the green colored variant to show fill progress.

A solid five-pointed star filled bright green with a darker green outline and a subtle inner shading, flat vector cartoon UI style. Ideal as a filled rating star, level score pip, achievement marker, or collectible icon in a game HUD or results screen.

A five-pointed star rendered in light grey/white as an outline/empty version, flat vector style with a white border and pale grey fill. Represents an unearned or empty rating star, used alongside the filled green star for level ratings or progress displays.

A light grey five-pointed star with a subtle 3D depth/bevel effect (darker grey shading along the bottom edges), flat vector UI style. Works as an empty or disabled rating star with a pressed/inset look for score screens and achievement UIs.

A large green triangular arrow pointing right (east), flat vector style with a darker green rounded outline and a lighter green inner face giving a slight bevel. Typical use as a 'next', 'play', or move-right button icon in menus, carousels, or directional controls.

A smaller version of the green right-pointing triangular arrow, flat vector style with dark green border and lighter inner fill. Suited for compact UI elements like spinner/stepper controls, pagination, or small next buttons.

A large green triangular arrow pointing up (north), flat vector style with darker green outline and a beveled lighter inner triangle. Useful as an up/increase button, scroll-up indicator, or jump control in game UI.
A small green upward-pointing triangular arrow with dark green border, flat vector UI style. Fits compact spinners, sort indicators, or small scroll-up buttons in menus and inventory lists.

A large green triangular arrow pointing down (south), flat vector style with a darker green rounded outline and lighter beveled face. Used for down/decrease buttons, dropdown indicators, or scroll-down controls in game interfaces.
A small green downward-pointing triangular arrow with dark green outline, flat vector style. Good for compact dropdown carets, sort-descending indicators, or small scroll-down buttons.

A large green triangular arrow pointing left (west), flat vector style with darker green rounded border and lighter inner bevel. Typical 'back', 'previous', or move-left button icon for menus, carousels, and navigation.
A small green left-pointing triangular arrow with dark green outline, flat vector UI style. Suited to compact back buttons, pagination previous controls, or stepper decrement icons.

A large green stylized arrowhead/chevron pointing right (east) with a concave tail edge giving a sleek dart shape, flat vector style with darker green outline and lighter beveled fill. A more decorative alternative to the basic triangle for next/forward navigation or direction markers.
A small green decorative right-pointing arrowhead with concave back edge, flat vector style with dark green border. Works as a compact forward/next indicator or list bullet in stylized game menus.

A large green decorative arrowhead pointing up (north) with a notched concave bottom edge forming a dart/chevron shape, flat vector style with darker outline and lighter bevel. Suits stylized up-navigation, jump indicators, or upgrade markers.

A large green decorative arrowhead pointing down (south) with a notched concave top edge, like a downward chevron/dart, flat vector style with darker green outline. Useful as a stylized dropdown, download, or descend indicator in game UI.
A small green decorative downward arrowhead with notched top edge, flat vector style. Compact chevron for dropdown carets, minimize buttons, or downward direction hints.

A large green decorative arrowhead pointing left (west) with a concave right edge forming a dart shape, flat vector style with darker outline and lighter beveled fill. Stylized back/previous navigation icon for menus and carousels.

A wide rounded-rectangle button with a light grey/white face and a thin green border outline, flat vector UI style at double resolution. The neutral face with colored rim suits a secondary or unselected menu button that pairs with solid green primary buttons; 9-slice friendly.

A wide green rounded-rectangle button with a smooth vertical gradient from lighter to darker green and a dark depth edge along the bottom, flat vector style. Raised gradient-styled primary button for menu and dialog actions.

A wide light grey/white rounded-rectangle button with a thin green inner outline and a darker depth edge at the bottom, flat vector style. Raised outlined/secondary button variant, useful for cancel or unselected options next to solid green buttons.

A wide green rounded-rectangle button with a lighter glossy sheen across the upper half, flat vector cartoon style without a depth edge. Shiny flat button for menus, or pressed state for the glossy depth variant.

A circular button with a light grey/white face and a green ring border, flat vector UI style. Neutral round button suited for icon buttons (settings, close, pause) in a green-themed interface, reading as unselected or secondary.
